title: Vegan carrot cake
subtitle: Moist, spiced loaf with a lemon drizzle
excerpt: A vegan carrot cake made with flaxseed egg and vegetable oil that is just as moist as the dairy original. Cinnamon, ginger, and walnuts throughout; lemon drizzle on top.
sourceNotes: Carrot cake as a concept spans many traditions, from medieval European spiced fruit cakes through to the American cream-cheese frosted version that became ubiquitous after the 1960s. This vegan adaptation replaces eggs with flaxseed and dairy with plant milk, following techniques developed in Isa Chandra Moskowitz's Veganomicon (2007) and Meera Sodha's East (2019).

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Slice and freeze without the drizzle for up to 2 months. Drizzle after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake keeps well for 3 days in an airtight tin. The flavour improves on day two.

baking.decoratingTechnique: lemon drizzle poured over while warm

glossaryTerms.definition: A vegan egg substitute made by mixing ground flaxseed with water and leaving it for 5 minutes to gel. One flax egg (1 tablespoon flaxseed plus 3 tablespoons water) binds batter in the same way as a whole egg.

content.text: Oil-based cakes stay moist longer than butter-based ones, which is one reason a vegan carrot cake can be just as good as the original. The flaxseed egg gels in water and binds the batter without affecting the flavour. Grate the carrot coarsely; fine gratings disappear into the crumb and lose their texture.
Squeeze excess moisture from the grated carrot in a clean tea towel before weighing it out. Too much water in the batter can prevent the cake setting in the centre.
Mix the {{flaxseed-ground}} with the cold {{water}} in a small bowl or cup. Stir well and set aside for at least 5 minutes. The mixture will thicken to a gel. This is the
that binds the batter in place of eggs.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 900 g loaf tin. Whisk the {{plain-flour}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{ginger-ground}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} together in a large bowl. In a separate bowl, mix the flax egg with the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{oat-milk}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Add the wet ingredients to the dry and fold together until almost combined. Add the grated {{carrot}} and {{walnuts}} and fold until evenly distributed. Pour into the tin and bake for 50 to 55 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean.

While the cake bakes, stir the {{icing-sugar}} into the {{lemon-juice}} to make a thin, pourable glaze. As soon as the cake comes out of the oven, poke all over with a skewer and pour the glaze slowly over the top, letting it soak in. Leave to cool in the tin for 20 minutes before turning out onto a rack.
